title: Eighth-Note Root Groove
description: Play roots on every eighth note — twice as many notes per beat as the quarter-note groove. Count '1-and-2-and-3-and-4-and' out loud while you play. This pattern drives rock and pop bass lines.
instructions: Count out loud: '1-and-2-and-3-and-4-and' before you play a single note.
Play the A root (open A string) on every count — both the numbered beats and the 'ands'.
Alternate your plucking fingers (index, middle, index, middle) for even tone.
Tap your foot on 1-2-3-4 only (the quarter note pulse), while your hand plays the eighths. This split coordination is essential.
Stay relaxed — tension is the enemy of groove.

variations.label: Musical: mix quarter and eighth notes — quarters on 1 and 3, eighths on 2 and 4
Harder: eighth notes through I-IV-V in A

ensembleNotes: Eighth-note bass lines are the workhorse of rock and pop. Listen to 'Come As You Are' (Nirvana), 'Seven Nation Army' (White Stripes), or virtually any Beatles bass line — straight eighth notes driving the song forward. Simple, but it has to be rock-solid.
